name: Kuaokala Ridge Trail
tagline: A remote Waianae ridge trail with views that rival anything on the island — and almost no one there.
parking: Free parking at the Kuaokala Game Management Area access road off Farrington Highway — the gate is sometimes locked, check DLNR for access
hours: Open sunrise to sunset; access gate may be locked weekdays — check Hawaii DLNR schedule
bestTime: Weekend mornings when the access gate is reliably open
talkStory: This ridge trail runs through land jointly managed by the US Army and DLNR — Makua Valley below was used for live-fire training from WWII through 2004, and unexploded ordnance remains off-trail. The valley is considered the birthplace of humanity in some Hawaiian traditions, adding weight to the stunning scenery. Access gate may be locked weekdays — check DLNR's schedule. Connects to Kealia Trail for an ambitious ridge-to-ridge through-hike.
